shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc 'mempal search "Next.js server action cache tags posts revalidateTag updateTag stale content" --json --top-k 5'
/bin/bash -lc 'git diff -- app/actions.ts app/page.tsx lib/posts.ts next.config.ts tsconfig.json data/posts.json'

/bin/bash -lc 'cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/nextjs-update-tag-cache-components.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Next.js read-your-writes tag invalidation uses updateTag

In Next.js 16 cache components, use "'`updateTag()` inside a Server Action when a mutation must not be followed by stale cached content. `revalidateTag(tag, '"'max')"'` uses stale-while-revalidate semantics, while `updateTag(tag)` immediately expires the tagged cache so the next request blocks on fresh data. If a cached function uses the `"use cache"` directive, `cacheComponents: true` must be enabled in `next.config.ts`.
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'

errors: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
